reasoning: Seychelles is a small, politically stable island nation with a population of about 100,000. It has no ongoing civil conflicts, insurgencies, or significant organized armed groups. The ACLED dataset defines "Battles" as violent interactions between two organized armed groups. The reference value is listed as exactly 1.000, which confirms there have been zero battles in the 360 days preceding March 16, 2025. For the resolution criteria to be met, Seychelles would need to experience more than 10 times this baseline (i.e., at least 11 battles) within a single 30-day period leading up to any of the resolution dates. Experiencing 11 separate battles between organized armed groups in a single month would represent a sudden, full-scale civil war or an unprecedented level of violent maritime clashes (e.g., piracy). The likelihood of such a black-swan event occurring is vanishingly small. I assign the minimum allowable probabilities, slightly increasing over the forecast horizons purely to reflect general increasing temporal uncertainty.
